title: Choose batch or streaming by latency need
description: Default to batch for its simplicity and reprocessability; adopt streaming only when end-to-end latency truly requires it.
body: Use when deciding a pipeline's processing model. Do not reach for streaming because it sounds modern — streaming adds stateful operators, exactly-once complexity, and harder backfills for latency most use cases do not need. Actions: quantify the required freshness; if minutes-to-hours suffice, use scheduled batch with idempotent partitions; if seconds matter, use streaming with explicit dedup and watermarking. Validation signals include meeting the freshness SLO and a batch path that is trivially replayable. Risk boundary: streaming's operational burden is easy to underestimate; before committing, confirm the latency requirement is real and the team can run stateful infrastructure.
